problem_summary: 2017 MCM Problem D operations research: reduce TSA security wait time and its variance, find bottlenecks, recommend staffing. Team 56632 uses a two-stage parallel queuing network (ID check then scan, precheck and regular lanes), closed-form expected waits validated by a discrete-event simulation, cost-benefit staffing optimization, and a virtual-queue discipline test.
models_used: Two-stage tandem queuing network, ID-check then scan, parallel precheck and regular lanes, FIFO
Poisson arrivals estimated by MLE, KDE for service times (MATLAB expfit, ksdensity)
Precheck scan time as max of two independent variables, density from product of CDFs
Closed-form solved wait recurrence with three boxed average-wait formulas
Discrete-event simulation via dynamic programming, service times sampled from KDEs
Cost-benefit profit-rate optimization over station counts, plus FIFO vs Priority via virtual queue
key_techniques: Operationalize inefficiency as one metric, average wait time
Closed form first then validate by independent simulation overlay
Drop max-with-zero for a solvable recurrence, restore in simulation
Derive arrival-equals-service bottleneck threshold, overlay on curves
Nonparametric KDE plus parametric exponential where justified
Analyze variance not just mean; report the failed Priority discipline honestly
why_it_won: Mathematical depth plus honesty: real stochastic-process work (Poisson, MLE, KDE, order statistics, a solved recurrence) validated against an independent simulation; an elegant arrival-equals-service threshold confirmed empirically; candor about simplifications; a failed hypothesis turned into a recommendation; full prompt coverage including throughput, variance, cost with dollar figures, and behavior.
section_highlights: Four-part architecture stated up front; each assumption justified by its own remark; three boxed wait results; reproducible labeled pseudo-code; figures with predicted thresholds overlaid on observed transitions; a structured future-work list; real citations (Chen and Yao, an EJOR virtual-queuing paper, TSA statistics).
pitfalls_or_limitations: Closed-form waits can go negative from dropping max-with-zero, fixed only in simulation
Saturated-queue assumption never reaches steady state
Constant daily arrival rate ignores flight peaks, which the team admits
Security constraint asserted but never modeled
Profit objective coarse; slow-fast multiplier uncalibrated
reusable_patterns: Define one scalar metric early and justify it
Pair a closed-form model with an independent simulation
Flag a simplifying assumption then relax it numerically
Derive a load-equals-one threshold and overlay on data
Use KDE plus selective parametric fits estimated by MLE
Report failed hypotheses; use boxed equations and pseudo-code
